purpose: Validation & Decision Framework (VDF) for NeoGenomics accessioning: a deterministic, configuration-driven rule engine (WHEN/DECISION/ON SUCCESS/ON FAILURE, 6 operators, 5 outcome groups) with versioned rules, full decision-trace explainability, NL authoring copilot, and a governed, DATA-DRIVEN ENTITY SCHEMA REGISTRY as the bottom-up source of truth (entities with typed fields -> vocabulary projected from it -> incoming transactions validated against it -> rules reference guaranteed-typed fields). REBUILDING in Node/TypeScript; the proven .NET stack (src/backend) is the baseline, retained until the Node build reaches verified parity, then retired.

goals.title: Production enterprise-grade quality
goals.description: Clean module boundaries, full TS typing (strict), structured logging, graceful errors, input validation (Ajv/zod at boundaries), idempotency, observability, documented APIs.

goals.title: Security & vulnerability verification
goals.description: AuthN/AuthZ (JWT+RBAC) on every endpoint, input validation, parameterized data access (Prisma), secrets via .env/env only (never hard-coded; incl. OPENAI_API_KEY, JWT secret, DB creds), OWASP review, npm audit clean (no high/critical), no PHI/secrets in logs, schema-validated LLM output. Findings block closure.

goals.title: Rich UI/UX design quality
goals.description: Reuse + repoint the existing polished React/Fluent UI v9 app; adapt the vocabulary screen to the entity-registry model. WCAG 2.1 AA.

conventions.dotnet_baseline: src/backend (proven; retained until Node parity, then removed)
reused_artifacts: rule.schema.json — the Node engine must reproduce identical outcomes on this corpus (cross-validation vs .NET)
conventions.postgres: host=localhost port=5433 (docker); Node uses database 'iawnode' (separate from .NET's 'iaw')
conventions.principle: Entity Schema Registry is the bottom-up source of truth. Deterministic runtime; NL authoring compile-time only. Configure-over-code: entities, fields, rules, vocabulary are all data, governed at runtime — no redeploy to extend the model.
conventions.llm_config: OpenAI via .env (OPENAI_API_KEY, OPENAI_MODEL=gpt-4.1, OPENAI_BASE_URL); gitignored; never hard-coded.

features.description: N0 Server scaffold: NestJS app (src/server), Prisma + Postgres(iawnode), config from .env, JWT auth + RBAC (Author/Reviewer/Admin) via guards, /health, global exception filter (RFC7807-style), structured logging (no PHI/secrets), Swagger, CORS for the UI, Vitest + supertest harness.
features.verified_by: build 0 err; 16 Jest+supertest; audit 0 high/crit; live health/swagger/login

features.description: N1 Entity Schema Registry (bottom-up source of truth): Prisma entities + fields tables (entity key case-insensitive unique, fields with name/type/required/allowedValues/description); registry service (governed CRUD, add-entity + add-field-by-selection — no free-text objects); compile each entity to a JSON Schema; Ajv validation of a transaction/fact document against the registry; seeded from the canonical entities (derived from the .NET vocabulary subjects). Admin API + tests.
acceptance: entities+fields persisted; entity keys case-insensitively unique (no Kit/kit dup)
Ajv validates a fact doc against the registry (shape/type/allowedValues)
features.verified_by: 45 tests; live Kit->201/KIT->409 case-insensitive dedup; bad-fact validation type+enum errors
features.notes: The new foundation; free-text-object/Kit-kit bug structurally impossible.

features.description: N2 VDF core engine (TS): fact resolution over JSON, 6 operator families, leaf/group conditions + quantifiers, 5 outcome groups, recovery, rule-chaining (derive phase), reconciler, decision traces. Pure TS, embeddable. MUST reproduce identical outcomes on the reused rules/*.json + fixtures/*.json corpus (cross-validate vs .NET).
acceptance: the corpus (10 reference + extension rules) produces the SAME outcomes as the .NET build over the fixtures

features.description: N3 Rule persistence + repository: Prisma rules/rule_versions/reference_data/decision_traces (versioned, effective-dated, rule body JSONB); reference-data provider; corpus importer; vocabulary PROJECTED from the entity registry (objects=entities, properties=fields) — not a separate free-text list. Integration tests against iawnode.
acceptance: vocabulary is a projection of the registry

features.description: N4 Authoring tooling: rule JSON-schema validation (Ajv on rule.schema.json), vocabulary linter grounding on the registry (subjects must be real entity.field; type checks), round-trip paraphraser, dry-run previewer (real engine, read-only). Tests.
acceptance: linter rejects unknown/typo entity.field + type mismatches

features.description: N5 OpenAI NL interpreter: constrained NL->structured rule grounded in the registry-projected vocabulary; Ajv+lint validation gate (no silent invention; gaps surfaced); OPENAI_* from .env; deterministic stub for offline/tests.
acceptance: NL compiles to rule using only registry terms
output validated; invalid rejected as gaps

features.description: N6 API surface (NestJS): POST /evaluate (validate facts against registry, then evaluate -> outcomes+trace), authoring endpoints, rule governance CRUD (version/approve/effective-date), entity-registry admin (entities/fields), vocabulary projection. JWT+RBAC, Swagger, health, error filter. supertest integration tests.

N7 Repoint React UI to the Node API; adapt the Vocabulary screen to the entity-registry model: manage Entities and their typed Fields (add entity; add field by selecting the entity, not free-text path; field type + allowedValues); authoring scope + repository 'operates on' read from the registry. WCAG AA.
acceptance: UI works against Node API end-to-end

features.description: N8 Hardening + retire .NET: security review (G2), npm audit, perf (100+ rules within SLA), full regression, docs (README/architecture/integration/authoring/registry). Once Node is at verified parity, remove src/backend (.NET) and update docs/bootstrap.
